Home › Guides

Money guides & explainers

61 plain-English guides to the money decisions that matter — no jargon, no sales pitch, just the reasoning and the numbers.

No guides match — try another word.

"What Is a 401(k) Match, and Why It's the Best Deal in Finance" How employer 401(k) matching works, why skipping it means leaving free money on the table, and how vesting and contribution limits affect you. "The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. "APR vs APY: The Difference That Costs (or Earns) You Money" APR and APY look alike but mean different things. Understand compounding, why lenders quote APR and banks quote APY, and how to compare fairly. "How to Build Wealth in Your 20s and 30s" The moves that matter most when you're young — why starting early beats everything, and a simple playbook that turns modest income into long-term wealth. "Buy Now, Pay Later: Convenience or Debt Trap?" How buy-now-pay-later really works, why 'interest-free' installments can still hurt your finances, and how to use BNPL without falling into the trap. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "How Credit Card Interest Really Works (and How to Pay Zero)" How credit card APR, daily compounding and the grace period actually work — why balances snowball, and the simple habit that lets you pay no interest at all. "What Actually Affects Your Credit Score" The five factors behind your credit score, which ones matter most, and the practical habits that raise it — without the myths. "Debt-to-Income Ratio: The Number Lenders Care About Most" What your debt-to-income ratio is, why lenders use it to decide loans and mortgages, the thresholds that matter, and how to improve yours. "Diversification: Why You Shouldn't Bet Everything on One Thing" What diversification is, why it's called the only free lunch in investing, and how to spread risk across companies, asset types and geographies the simple way. "Do You Need Life Insurance? Term vs Whole Life, Explained" Who actually needs life insurance, how much, and why term insurance beats whole life for most people. A clear guide to a confusing, heavily-sold product. "Dollar-Cost Averaging: Why Boring, Automatic Investing Wins" How dollar-cost averaging works, what the research actually says versus lump-sum investing, and why the biggest benefit is behavioral, not mathematical. "How Big Should Your Emergency Fund Be? (And Where to Keep It)" How to size your emergency fund for your situation, where to keep it so it's safe but accessible, and how to build one without derailing other goals. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "Estate Planning Basics: Why Everyone Needs a Plan (Not Just the Rich)" What estate planning really involves — wills, beneficiaries, powers of attorney — why it matters at any wealth level, and the simple steps to protect your family. "ETFs vs Mutual Funds: Which Should You Choose?" The real differences between ETFs and mutual funds — trading, fees, taxes and minimums — and why for most long-term investors it barely matters which you pick. "The Financial Order of Operations: What to Do With Your Money First" A clear, prioritized sequence for every spare dollar — emergency fund, employer match, high-interest debt, investing — so you never wonder what comes next. "The FIRE Movement Explained: Financial Independence by the Numbers" What FIRE really means, the 25x rule and 4% withdrawal, the variants (Lean, Fat, Coast, Barista FIRE), and the one number that decides your timeline. "First-Time Home Buyer Guide: From Saving to Closing" A step-by-step guide for first-time buyers — how much to save, what you can afford, the full costs, and the process from pre-approval to getting the keys. "Good Debt vs Bad Debt: A Simple Framework for Borrowing Decisions" Not all debt is equal. A practical framework for telling wealth-building debt from wealth-destroying debt, using interest rate, asset value and cash flow. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "How Much Should You Save for Retirement?" Rules of thumb, the real math behind retirement targets, and how to turn a scary abstract goal into a monthly number you can actually act on. "How Much Should You Spend on a Car? Rules of Thumb That Actually Work" Practical guidelines for a sensible car budget — the 20/4/10 rule, why total cost dwarfs the sticker price, and how a car quietly drains wealth. "How to Build Credit From Scratch (and Fix a Low Score)" A practical, step-by-step guide to building a credit history from zero and raising a low score — what actually moves the needle and what to ignore. "How to Handle a Market Crash Without Wrecking Your Finances" What to do — and what never to do — when markets fall. Why crashes are normal, why selling locks in losses, and how to turn a downturn to your advantage. "How to Make a Budget That Actually Sticks" A practical guide to budgeting that survives real life — the main methods compared, how to set one up in an afternoon, and why most budgets fail. "How to Negotiate a Raise (Without the Awkwardness)" A practical, evidence-based guide to asking for a raise — how to build your case, what number to name, the words to use, and why it compounds over a career. "How to Prepare for a Recession (Before It Arrives)" Practical steps to recession-proof your finances — strengthen your safety net, income, and debt position now, and why panic-selling investments is the wrong move. "Index Funds Explained: Why the Boring Choice Beats the Pros" What an index fund is, why it quietly outperforms most active managers after fees, and how to use one as the core of a simple, low-cost portfolio. "10 Investing Myths That Keep People Poor" The most common investing myths — from 'you need to be rich to start' to 'timing the market works' — debunked with the evidence, so you can invest with confidence. "Lifestyle Creep: Why Earning More Doesn't Make You Richer" How lifestyle inflation quietly eats every raise, why high earners can still live paycheck to paycheck, and a simple rule to keep more of what you earn. "How to Manage Money as a Couple (Without Fighting About It)" Practical approaches to combining finances with a partner — joint vs separate accounts, handling different incomes, and the conversations that prevent money fights. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "A Money Checklist for Every Decade of Life (20s to 60s)" The key financial moves for each stage of life — from building habits in your 20s to preparing for retirement in your 60s — in one clear roadmap. "Why Net Worth Is the Only Number That Really Matters" Income tells you how fast you're rowing; net worth tells you where the boat actually is. How to calculate it, track it, and use it to build wealth. "Opportunity Cost: The Hidden Price Tag on Every Money Decision" The most important concept in economics, applied to your money — why every dollar and hour has a hidden cost, and how thinking in trade-offs makes you richer. "Passive Income: What Actually Works vs. the Hype" An honest look at passive income — why most 'passive' income isn't passive, which approaches genuinely build hands-off cash flow, and the scams to avoid. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament. "Should You Pay Off Your Mortgage Early? The Honest Answer" The math and the psychology of paying off a mortgage early versus investing — when each wins, and why the 'right' answer is partly emotional. "How to Price Freelance Work Without Underselling Yourself" Why new freelancers charge too little, how to calculate a rate that actually sustains you, and when to move from hourly to value-based pricing. "The Psychology of Money: Behavioral Biases That Quietly Cost You" Money decisions are emotional, not just mathematical. The most common behavioral biases that sabotage investors and savers — and how to defend against them. "Rental Property Investing Basics: The Numbers That Matter" A beginner's guide to judging a rental property — cap rate, cash flow, cash-on-cash return, the expenses rookies forget, and where the real returns come from. "Renting vs Buying a Home: The Honest Math" Beyond 'rent is throwing money away' — the real costs on both sides, the break-even horizon, and how to decide based on your actual situation. "Roth vs Traditional: Which Retirement Account Wins?" A clear framework for choosing between Roth and Traditional retirement accounts — the tax logic, the break-even rule, and why many people use both. "How to Save for a House Down Payment (Without It Taking Forever)" How much to save for a down payment, where to keep it, and practical tactics to get there faster — plus why 20% isn't always the right target. "Saving vs Investing: What's the Difference and When to Do Each" Saving and investing are not the same thing. What sets them apart, why you need both, and a simple rule for deciding which to do with any given dollar. "Sequence of Returns Risk: Why When You Retire Matters as Much as How Much" The retirement danger hiding inside average returns — why a crash early in retirement is far worse than the same crash later, and how to defend against it. "How to Set Financial Goals You'll Actually Achieve" Why vague money goals fail and specific ones succeed — how to set SMART financial goals, prioritize them, and turn each into an automatic monthly number. "Sinking Funds: The Simple Trick for Big Expenses" How sinking funds turn irregular, budget-wrecking expenses into small predictable monthly savings — and why they beat relying on your emergency fund. "How to Start Investing With Little Money: A Beginner's Roadmap" You don't need thousands to start investing. A practical, jargon-free roadmap for beginners — what to do first, what to buy, and the mistakes to skip. "Stocks vs Bonds: How to Think About Asset Allocation" What stocks and bonds actually are, how they behave differently, and how to choose a stock/bond mix that matches your timeline and stomach for risk. "How to Stop Living Paycheck to Paycheck" A practical, judgment-free plan to break the paycheck-to-paycheck cycle — even on a modest income — by creating breathing room and building it into a buffer. "How to Survive a Job Loss Financially" A calm, practical action plan for losing your job — what to do in the first days, how to stretch your money, and how to protect your long-term finances. "How to Teach Kids About Money (by Age)" Age-by-age ideas for raising money-smart kids — from piggy banks to first jobs and investing — building the habits that schools rarely teach. "The Time Value of Money: Why a Dollar Today Beats a Dollar Tomorrow" The single most important idea in finance, explained plainly — present value, future value, discounting, and why it underpins every investment and loan decision. "The True Cost of Owning a Car (It's Not the Payment)" Depreciation, insurance, fuel, maintenance and financing — the full cost of car ownership, and how to spend far less on getting around. "Understanding Inflation: How It Quietly Shrinks Your Money" What inflation is, why central banks target it, how it erodes cash and helps borrowers, and how to protect your money from losing value over time. "Understanding Your Paycheck: Where Your Money Actually Goes" A line-by-line guide to your pay stub — gross vs net, taxes, and deductions — so you know exactly why your take-home is less than your salary. "Value-Based Spending: Frugality Without Feeling Deprived" How to spend lavishly on what you love and cut hard on what you don't — a sustainable alternative to restrictive budgeting that actually makes you happier. "What to Do With a Windfall: Bonus, Inheritance, or Lump Sum" A calm, step-by-step plan for handling a sudden sum of money — from a bonus to an inheritance — so it builds lasting wealth instead of vanishing. "When Does Refinancing Your Mortgage Make Sense?" How to tell whether refinancing will save you money — the break-even rule, the costs people forget, and the traps that make a lower rate a worse deal.
FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

About Methodology Contact Privacy Policy Terms of Use

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
